figures.caption: Fig. 2: Network architecture of MD-Net. It consists of a gating network and an expert network, with the final output being n semantic vectors w1,w2, . . . ,wn. These semantic vectors are aligned with the corresponding SBVs b1,b2, . . . ,bn through a geometry-aware loss LGA. The image generator part uses a pre-trained GAN network.
TABLE I: Quantitative comparison of AA and IDS metrics for various facial attribute editing methods based on the ProGAN and StyleGAN model.

TABLE II: Quantitative comparison of FID and LPIPS metrics for various facial attribute editing methods based on ProGAN and StyleGAN model (bold numbers indicate the best results, and underlined values denote the second-best results).
Fig. 4: Qualitative evaluation of MD-Face and diffusion-based baseline methods across the age, gender, and smile attributes.
Fig. 5: Qualitative ablation results for MD-Face, comparing the full model with variants without LGA and LPPA.

Fig. 3: Qualitative comparison of facial attribute editing across different generative models. Each subfigure demonstrates three editing operations. The vertical arrangement allows for direct comparison of editing performance across model architectures.
